sections.about: This metric measures sales to related parties as a percentage of total sales. It is a Section A 'Openness of Business' related-party disclosure, linked to Principle 1 transparency, fair dealing, and conflict-of-interest management. A lower value indicates better performance because it suggests a smaller proportion of revenue is concentrated within the related-party ecosystem.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: In FY2022-23, SAIL led with the lowest share at 0.04%, followed by Tata at 11%, Jindal at 20%, and JSW at 34.1%. In FY2023-24, SAIL remained the leader at 0.06%, Tata held at 12%, Jindal improved to 17%, and JSW improved to 30.7%. In FY2024-25, SAIL continued to lead at 0.08%, Tata was at 13.04%, Jindal improved further to 15.91%, and JSW worsened to 34.76%. SAIL's minimal related-party sales ratio reflects its PSU structure where the majority of steel products are sold to external government and commercial buyers, with very limited routing through group entities. Jindal's improving trend across three years reflects a shift toward more direct external sales. JSW and Tata's higher ratios reflect group distribution, service centre, and trading entity structures where related-party channels form a material portion of the sales mix. Sales-side RPT concentration can arise from downstream distribution companies, service centres, group trading arms, or captive consumption entities.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranks 2nd at 13.04%, behind SAIL at 0.08% and ahead of Jindal at 15.91% and JSW at 34.76%. Tata's ratio has risen modestly from 11% in FY2022-23 to 12% in FY2023-24 and 13.04% in FY2024-25, indicating a slight increase in related-party sales concentration. Despite this, Tata holds a consistent 2nd-place rank across all three years. Management should position this as a relative strength versus JSW and ensure downstream related-party sales channels are priced at arm's length and periodically benchmarked.
sections.comparabilityNotes: FY2022-23 values for all four companies are prior-year comparatives reported in their respective FY2023-24 BRSRs. Related-party sales are sensitive to whether downstream distribution, service centres, trading arms, and export entities are consolidated within the listed entity or treated as related parties outside the reporting boundary.
